descriptionHeader: Tranquil Luxury with Unimpeded Ocean Views
description:
Dual Mandate.

Immerse yourself in a slice of paradise where rocks and fynbos create a stunning natural garden, just steps away from the soft sandy shores. This thoughtfully designed home appears to be cradled by the water's edge, providing an unparalleled connection to the ocean. Adding to the property’s allure, a lush greenbelt ensures the utmost privacy with no neighbors on one side, allowing you to savor the serenity of your surroundings.

As you enter, you're greeted by breathtaking ocean views that set the tone for ultimate relaxation and tranquility. The open layout enhances the flow between the spacious bedrooms and inviting living areas, ensuring comfort and ease for family and guests alike.

Elegant double front doors lead into a grand entrance hall, transitioning seamlessly to an impressive home office and the main lounge. Stylish wooden stacker doors offer the flexibility to separate the formal lounge from the well-appointed, cottage-style kitchen and dining area. The family room, also featuring stacker doors, provides a bright and airy space that connects the heart of the home to the beauty of the sea.

The master suite is a private sanctuary, located in its own tranquil wing. This spacious retreat boasts stunning views of the ocean and garden, a generously sized dressing room with floor-to-ceiling built-in cupboards, and a luxurious full bathroom featuring a double vanity. Bedroom two, with mountain views, is conveniently located off the left side of the study, while bedrooms three and four are nestled between the lounge and open plan living area, sharing a well-appointed full bathroom.

The property includes three extra-length automated garages, providing direct access to the kitchen and an additional bathroom perfect for rinsing off the sea salt and sand after a day of fun on the water.

A wrap-around sea-facing veranda offers generous seating areas to unwind and soak in the coastal vistas. Accessed conveniently from both the lounge and a separate entrance through the dining room, this outdoor oasis is perfect for entertaining or simply enjoying the soothing sounds of the ocean.

This remarkable home captures the essence of coastal living, combining elegance, functionality, and breathtaking views—making it a true dream come true.

Rooi Els is a small seaside village, comprising +/-316 properties itself a conservancy, and is located on the R44 Clarence Drive between Gordons Bay and Pringle Bay in the Western Cape province of SA.

description:
De Companjie Guesthouse - Where Time Stands Still:

Exclusive Joint Mandate

There’s something magical about places that carry the whispers of time.
Nestled beneath the shade of centuries-old oak trees, this 1832 national heritage guesthouse tells a story of elegance, love, and timeless South African charm.

Every detail feels intentional — the creak of the original yellowwood floors, the glow of antique Oregon beams, and the sense of calm that lingers in every room. Guests arrive as travelers but leave as storytellers, drawn in by the soul and beauty of Swellendam, the third-oldest town in the country.

This fully furnished and operational property holds consent use for both a Guest House and a Restaurant, offering the perfect turnkey opportunity for hospitality entrepreneurs or investors seeking a business with heritage, heart, and history.

Designed with comfort and functionality in mind, the layout thoughtfully separates private and guest areas. The main dwelling features two en-suite bedrooms on the upper level for owners or managers, while a guest suite on the lower level ensures effortless hosting. Four additional suites are annexed to the main house, three of which offer private off-street entrances, each opening onto an enclosed garden and lapa with a built-in braai — ideal for outdoor entertaining.

The property also features a fully appointed kitchen, thoughtfully designed for both daily living and guest service, complete with all essential appliances, cookware, and utensils. A separate scullery adds convenience and functionality, while a versatile outbuilding houses a dedicated laundry area, making operations seamless and effortless.

Guests can enjoy elegant communal spaces including a dedicated breakfast room, formal dining area, study, and guest restroom, all designed to complement the building’s original character while offering modern convenience.

With seven en-suite guest rooms, each uniquely styled with luxurious finishes and king-size beds, De Companjie blends nostalgia with contemporary comfort. Landscaped gardens and charming outdoor seating create a tranquil haven where history and hospitality meet.

This is more than a guesthouse.
It’s a living legacy — ready for its next chapter and its next custodian. 

Arrange a private viewing today and experience the magic of De Companjie for yourself.

Price excludes VAT.

descriptionHeader: Luxury Meets Opportunity: Rooiels Coastal Haven with Guesthouse Potential
description:
On the slopes of Rooiels, this exquisite property offers an unparalleled blend of luxury living and commercial potential. As you approach the home, you're immediately struck by its elegant architectural design, seamlessly integrating with the natural beauty of its surroundings.

The home boasts spectacular, uninterrupted views of both the mountain ranges and the vast expanse of the ocean, creating a living panorama that changes with the light and seasons. Floor-to-ceiling windows throughout the property maximize these breathtaking vistas, allowing natural light to flood the interior spaces.

Stepping inside, you're greeted by a spacious open-plan living area that exudes sophistication and warmth. The high ceilings and thoughtful layout create an atmosphere of expansive comfort. 

The property features three large, luxurious bedrooms, each with its own ensuite bathroom. The master suite is a true retreat, offering a private balcony with panoramic views, a spacious walk-in closet, and a sumptuous bathroom. Two of the upstairs bedrooms are equipped with kitchenettes, adding versatility and convenience for guests or potential rental opportunities.

All bathrooms in the home showcase high-end fixtures, sleek, modern designs that blend functionality with indulgence. The attention to detail in these spaces reflects the overall quality and thoughtfulness of the home's design.

Outside, the property continues to impress with beautifully landscaped gardens that complement the natural terrain. The outdoor space of this Rooiels property is a masterpiece of a floral kingdom, seamlessly blending cultivated beauty with the raw splendor of the Cape's natural flora. As you step outside, you're greeted by a thoughtfully planned garden that respects and enhances the indigenous ecosystem while providing functional and aesthetic spaces for relaxation and entertainment.

Winding pathways of natural stone meander through the garden, inviting exploration and offering different vantage points to appreciate both the cultivated areas and the breathtaking sea and mountain views. These paths are lined with a carefully curated selection of water-wise, indigenous plants that thrive in the coastal climate. Swathes of colorful fynbos, including proteas, ericas, and restios, create a vibrant tapestry that changes with the seasons, attracting local birdlife and butterflies.

Multiple outdoor living spaces, including a covered patio and sun deck, provide perfect spots for alfresco dining, entertaining, or simply relaxing while taking in the incredible views.

Practical amenities have not been overlooked. The home features three garages, ensuring secure parking and additional storage. 

What sets this property apart is its commercial rights, opening up exciting possibilities for operating a boutique guesthouse. The layout of the home, with its multiple ensuite bedrooms and kitchenettes, is ideally suited for this purpose, allowing for a seamless transition between private residence and upscale accommodation business.

Every aspect of this home has been carefully considered to create a living experience that is both luxurious and functional. From its prime location and stunning views to its high-end finishes and thoughtful design, this property represents the pinnacle of upmarket coastal living in Rooiels, with the added benefit of commercial potential for those with an entrepreneurial spirit.

Whether used as a private sanctuary or a boutique guesthouse, this exceptional property offers a unique opportunity to own a piece of paradise and potentially turn it into a thriving business venture.

An elegant sweeping staircase leads you to the upper level, where two guest suites await:
Suite One: This suite boasts a sea-facing lounge with sliding doors opening to a private balcony, a convenient kitchenette with ample storage, a spacious bedroom with breathtaking views, and an ensuite bathroom.
Suite Two: The second suite features direct access to two expansive verandas, an ensuite bathroom, and a compact kitchenette.

As an added bonus, this exceptional property includes , three grey water tanks for eco-friendly water management, and ample storage space under the house. 

Seize this opportunity to own a stunning home that combines luxury and practicality, perfectly suited for families or as a lucrative rental option. Your coastal oasis awaits!

Descend the sculpturally striking spiral staircase to the second floor where the living happens, and discover another balcony,  living and dining area with a view, and a fireplace to warm up around. Two garages, outdoor braai and entertaining area, along with a garden sloping gently down to the shore complete this dream property at a perfect location.
Come to the coast,  where Whale-spotting, beautiful surroundings and fresh sea air are the order of the day.

descriptionHeader: Luxury Seafront Property in Kleinbaai, Overstrand, on the  popular Whale Coast Route of the Western Cape, South Africa.
description:
Once again, location, location! A dual-investment opportunity, being a residential home and a proven successful, year-round turn-key Air BnB. 2 hours' drive from Cape Town International Airport and 45 minutes' drive from Hermanus.

This immaculate, stunning, modern interior home with low maintenance, high end finishes and spectacular ocean views is literally within walking distance from the Kleinbaai Harbour, from where Marine Dynamics launch their Dyer Island tours, Shark Cage Diving tours, fishing expeditions, whale watching trips in Walker Bay, marine adventures and fine dining at the Great White House Restaurant. Penguine and wild bird sanctuary as well as Dallas Animal Farm is just up the road from Kleinbaai Harbour.

Situated in a protected natural zone where eco-tourism is increasing exponentially, demand for luxury lifestyle living is in growing demand. Kleinbaai is part of the Cape Floral Kingdom, where a diverse number of flowers adorn the fields and pavements, sand dunes and gardens during the winter and early spring months. Wild geese roam the town and beaches (from hence the town's name Gansbaai, jokingly referred to as "Goosebay" by the locals) and raise their chicks at the beginning of September - October annually - #cuteness overload.  

This valuable home boasts 5 bedrooms, 2.5 bathrooms, a self-contained studio apartment with ensuite and designer kitchenette, 2 covered parkings, a large open plan chef's kitchen, entertainment area, with stack doors ensuring seamless indoor-outdoor flow, gas geyser, study, large garden and optionally includes all furnishings and appliances. A move-in and stay property, not to be missed.

Will you invest or loose out? Call for your viewing approintment right away!

Adventure activities in the area include horse riding (Gansbaai / Pearly Beach), zip-lining (Caledon), private game reserves, wine tasting, fishing, sun, fun and good food... 



Cape Agulhas (the most southern point of Africa where the Indian Ocean meets the Atlantic Ocean) is about 95km (1 hour 30 minutes' drive) away. The historic town of Stanford is 15 km's drive from De Kelders, where an annual flower show is exhibited, a variety of cuisine is found (from Mexican to traditional local food, Italian food, fine dining, etc) and art galleries proudly exhibit masterpieces.

This coastal haven, ideal as a holiday or permanent residential home, promises endless opportunities. descriptionHeader: Excellent Farm In Overberg
description:
Sole Mandate. 
What a privilege to market this particular grain farm. The beautiful Overberg is winter grain world. It's also sheep and cattle world.  This 102 ha farm is situated approximately 5 km from the village of Tesselaarsdal, 22 km from Caledon, 38 km from Hermanus and Stanford and 145 km from Cape Town.
Winter grains such as wheat, barley and oats and also a lot of canola are grown nowadays. Then grazing crops are also planted. The fertile soil offers a variety of possibilities. The other farming branch is of course the life stock, cattle and sheep.
Virtually all of the land is arable with very little that cannot be utilized, such as roads, dams and small gorges. There are two fairly large dams and also smaller dams for life stock drinking. 
There are currently no buildings on the farm. Picturesque views can be enjoyed from the higher lying areas! There are beautiful places where you can build your home. This farm offers the opportunity to develop it into a beautiful farm.
At present, the farm is rented out. Should the buyer be interested, the tenant can just proceed with the rent. However, the tenant still has the next winter season to complete his lease until December. 
Now is a good time to invest in this developing area. The price is R14 500 000 Plus VAT if applicable
Feel free to contact us for any queries.
We walk the extra mile, come walk with us, lets talk!

descriptionHeader: A Heritage Haven on 5000 sqm – Where Onrus’ Early Story Still Lives
description:
SOLE MANDATE: This is a rare opportunity of Onrus history, for a discerning family. Cradled beneath a magnificent mountain backdrop and sea views on the front. Stretching over half a hectare of land. This cherished early 20th century farmhouse with its iconic twin gabled bays, central veranda and timeless corrugated roofline stands as a beautifully intact reminder of the area’s farming origins. The home’s welcoming front façade, framed by casement windows and a later 1930s veranda addition, immediately transports you to a quieter era. Here, life still slows to the rhythm of the land. Here, stories linger in sun-soaked timber, wood-framed windows, and the soft creak of an old floorboard.

Beyond the main house lies a quirky, character-filled cottage the perfect creative escape or guest retreat. A scattering of charming Wendy houses adds flexibility and delight, whether for workspaces, storage, hobbies, or play. Pure spring water flows from the mountains to the house alongside the municipal supply a quiet luxury many only dream of. There is a respected private school on the neighbouring property, a practical plus for families, and everything from Blue Flag Onrus Beach to Davies Pool, restaurants, galleries and shops is only about five minutes away. Whale Coast Mall, Hermanus CBD and a hospital are also within easy reach. It’s a place with soul a home that still carries the spirit of early agricultural settlement, a residence whose relationship with its surroundings is both meaningful and rare.

With its Grade 3B heritage status, the property is protected for the future, ensuring that its unique character remains treasured and respected. It has seen ongoing care and maintenance, and now awaits someone who values authenticity, history and the romance of a bygone era. Expansive land. A dignified homestead. An atmosphere you simply cannot recreate. Come discover a truly exceptional piece of Onrus past and perhaps your own future. Shape your own legacy. Be the next custodian.

descriptionHeader: Dairy farm and artisanal cheese operation for sale on the Klein River, Stanford, Western Cape
description:
An outstanding 245 ha working dairy farm and artisanal cheese operation on the Klein River outside Stanford is offered as a fully operational going concern at R 50 million, combining strong income streams with an exceptional lifestyle setting. 

Location and setting 
Prime rural position on Caledon Road in the Theewaterskloof Non-Urban area, a short drive from Stanford and within easy reach of Hermanus and Gansbaai. 
The 245.315 ha farm lies in a picturesque valley with rolling pastures, views of the Kleinriviersberg mountain, and the Klein River meandering through the property, offering natural river frontage and irrigation opportunities. 
Registered as Farm 1003, Theewaterskloof Municipality, with mixed-use zoning well suited to commercial farming and hospitality ventures. 
Income-producing farm and factory 
High-efficiency dairy with a 40-point swing-over Dairy Master parlour (designed for ±700 cows), 8,000 l bulk tank, 75 kVA generator, and supporting sheds for cattle, sheep, and pigs, enabling integrated livestock and whey-fed pig operations. 
Fully equipped artisanal cheese factory (±410 m²) with maturation and cold rooms, producing a range of award-winning farm cheeses from the on-farm herd, plus a butchery and cold-storage facilities for value-added meat processing. 
Productive arable land (±160 ha, with ±35 ha under irrigation) and young almond orchards, with scope for additional high-value crops such as vines (e.g. Shiraz), nuts or rotational cash crops. 

Water, power, and infrastructure 
Exceptional water security with multiple boreholes (five or more in production), several storage/irrigation dams, and registered water rights of approximately 200,000 m³ per annum from the river, supporting both irrigation and processing uses. 
The combination of borehole, dam, and river water provides year-round reliability, even in dry seasons, and underpins further expansion into crops or agri-tourism activities. 
Power is supplied via three 100 kVA Eskom points supplemented by on-site generators, ensuring uninterrupted operation of the dairy, factory, and accommodation. 

Residences and guest accommodation 
Elegant main manor house (±300 m²) with generous reception areas, farmhouse kitchen, scullery, and an adjoining ±85 m² games/entertainment room, set in an established garden. 
Additional residential units include a manager’s house (±85 m²) plus a bachelor flat and studio (±130 m²), ideal for senior staff or extended family. 
Five charming guest/farm-stay cottages – including Stone House, Mountain Cottage, River Cottage and Stable Cottage (ranging roughly 120–160 m²) – together with dedicated staff accommodation (±9 units), providing immediate income potential from self-catering stays, retreats or wedding groups. 

Lifestyle, agri-tourism, and buyer fit 
A rare blend of a serious production unit and relaxed country lifestyle: owners can step out from the manor house to river walks, birdlife and views, while the next generation focuses on dairy, cheese and diversified farming. 
Ideal for a family scaling down from a more intensive tourism/resort operation but still wanting an active, profitable farm with strong systems, space for adult children to farm, and a ready-made agri-tourism platform (farm shop, tastings, events, weddings, and farm-stay accommodation). 
Offered as a going concern at R 50 000 000 (if buyer is also a registered VAT vendor), presenting a substantial, income-generating rural investment in one of the Western Cape’s most sought-after farming districts.

This property offers more than just a home; it promises a lifestyle steeped in natural beauty, comfort, and modern conveniences. Located between Pringle Bay and Betty's Bay, this remarkable setting provides solitude, unspoiled nature, and breathtaking sea and mountain views.

Offering five comfortable, view-drenched bedrooms, three en-suite and a chef’s kitchen equipped with a Falcon stove and Caesarstone counter tops. For absolute comfort, the home includes underfloor heating and a captivating central gas fireplace—making this the perfect retreat, whether you're entertaining in the summer sun or enjoying a cozy winter storm.
The, open-plan lounge, dining, and family areas flow directly onto the outdoor decks, inviting the crisp sea air and invigorating scent inside. You are so close to the water, you can reach out and touch the rocks that lead to the crashing surf.

Below the majestic slopes of Hangklip mountain, Sea Farm is a share block company and limited to a maximum of twelve privileged shareholders. The coastline of the property measures approximately 6,5 km. It is a chance in a lifetime to enjoy an exclusive seaside lifestyle whilst making a unique investment in nature. The entire reserve is covered in fynbos and a large variety of plants and interspersed with wetlands and vlei. During late winter and early spring, the veld flowers are in full bloom, transforming the reserve into a wonderland of colour. The reserve is a prime whale-watching location, with abundant birdlife and wildlife.
The two private beaches offer excellent swimming and aquatic life. Each site has fresh water from the reserves own reservoir, and Eskom electricity supply. Access to the reserve is strictly controlled via electronic gate and there is also a resident full-time warden employed by the reserve.
